description_long: This is a heavy-duty concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. The barrier is rectangular with a sloped top edge and features two metal lifting hooks embedded on the upper surface for transport. Its surface is predominantly gray concrete, with a faded red stripe running horizontally along one side, and visible signs of wear, including chips, scuffs, and some graffiti or painted markings. The opposite side displays blue writing, likely for identification or placement instructions. The ends of the barrier are tapered, making it easier to interlock with adjacent barriers. The overall appearance suggests it has been used outdoors and exposed to the elements, resulting in a rough, weathered texture.
description: A weathered concrete jersey barrier with a faded red stripe, metal lifting hooks, and visible markings and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red stripe, two metal hooks, and surface wear.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow, tapered end and thickness of the barrier.
description_view_2: Back view with blue writing and less prominent red coloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the tapered end and some surface markings.
